content: The Verge The Verge is about technology and how it makes us feel. Founded in 2011, we offer our audience everything from breaking news to reviews to award-winning features and investigations, on our site, in video, and in podcasts. 2026-07-20T20:08:57+00:00 https://www.theverge.com/rss/index.xml https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2025/01/verge-rss-large_80b47e.png?w=150&h=150&crop=1 Brad Bourque https://www.theverge.com/?p=968191 2026-07-20T16:08:51-04:00 2026-07-20T16:08:51-04:00 Borderlands 3 is among the 15 games in the 2K Megahits 2026 Bundle. | 2K Games The aptly-named “2K Megahits 2026 Bundle” from Humble includes 15 Steam games for $15. It’s a smattering of the publisher 2K’s biggest games of the 2010s, including BioShock Infinite, Risk of Rain 2, Borderlands 3, and XCOM: Enemy Unknown . The bundle will be available until August 7th, though the bundle page notes that keys for some games are already starting to run low. All of the games work on Windows, while some support MacOS and SteamOS. 2K Megahits 2026 Bundle Where to Buy: $465 $15 at Humble One of my personal favorite games, Risk of Rain 2 , is included in the bundle. In the third-person roguelike, you’ll face off against increasingly difficult waves of enemies, fighting to stay alive as long as you can and collecting power-ups and useful perks before extracting to the next zone to do it all again. It’s just challenging enough to make victory satisfying, and fun to play alone or with friends. You also get 2012’s XCOM: Enemy Unknown , a legendary turn-based strategy game that’s usually $15 on its own, to give you a sense of the bundle’s value. The bundle also notably includes three games from the Borderlands universe. Borderlands 3 Special Edition is a traditional looter-shooter FPS, Tiny Tina’s Wonderlands adds RPG elements like an overworld map and spellcasting, and Tales from the Borderlands is an interactive episodic visual novel from Telltale Games. You’ll also get a variety of shooters, including BioShock Infinite, Duke Nukem 3D, Bulletstorm , plus Hidden and Dangerous from the turn of the millenium . Part of the proceeds from the bundle benefit Covenant House , an organization that benefits young people facing homelessness, as well as the publisher and Humble. You can adjust how you pledge is split between the beneficiaries by clicking the button that says “adjust donation” under the checkout button. You can find the full list of games in the bundle below: Risk of Rain 2 Borderlands 3 Standard Edition Mafia II: Definitive Edition BioShock Infinite Tiny Tina’s Wonderlands The Quarry Tribes of Midgard Deluxe Edition Homeworld Remastered Collection Tales from the Borderlands Bulletstorm Full Clip Edition Duke Nukem 3D: 20th Anniversary World Tour Hidden and Dangerous Bundle The Darkness II X-COM: UFO Defense XCOM Enemy Unknown ]]> Cameron Faulkner https://www.theverge.com/?p=967989 2026-07-20T16:06:40-04:00 2026-07-20T16:06:40-04:00 The ornament requires three 1.5-volt coin batteries, which are included in the box. | Photo: Cameron Faulkner/The Verge You probably know the signature PlayStation boot sound . Did you know that it's technically a multi-part chime? There's the synthy section where "Sony Computer Entertainment" shows onscreen with a white background. You only get to the next screen with the echo-y chimes and the color-filled PlayStation logo if your console recognizes the disc. How badly I wanted my sorta-broken console to get to that second screen. My console's disc spindle came apart pretty early into me owning it. As an eight-year-old kid, fixing the spindle was the riskiest undertaking of my life yet. The internet wasn't really a haven for how-tos yet, so I superglued that … Read the full story at The Verge. ]]> Andrew J. Hawkins https://www.theverge.com/?post_type=vm_stream&p=968205 2026-07-20T15:18:45-04:00 2026-07-20T15:18:45-04:00 The end of the Ford F-150 Lightning was also the start of a new era for the automaker. After failing to capture the market with the electric version of its popular F-Series truck, Ford is going back to the drawing board with its Universal Electric Vehicle Platform. The first offering will be a $30,000 electric truck, engineered for efficiency and also — perhaps most importantly — profitability. The truck will be revealed in 2027, but Ford plans on building a whole family of low-cost electric vehicles at its Kentucky assembly plant. The heavily camouflaged truck has already been spotted out in the wild, with observers noting that it seems even smaller than the Maverick. Success isn’t guaranteed. Ford has struggled to make profitable EVs, forcing the company to take a staggering $19.5 billion hit on its EV investments in 2025. But Ford still sees EVs as the future — just not oversize ones with “no path to profitability,” as executives say. Emojis as camouflage. Ford’s $30K electric truck could be smaller than a Maverick. Inside the lab where Ford is trying to crack the code on cheap EVs Ford’s EV and software chief Doug Field is leaving the company Ford is fighting against physics to build affordable EVs Ford’s big bet on EVs didn’t pan out — now it’s pivoting to hybrids and energy storage Ford CEO Jim Farley on China, tariffs, and the quest for a $30,000 EV Ford reveals breakthrough process for lower priced EVs ]]> Richard Lawler https://www.theverge.com/?p=968055 2026-07-20T16:08:57-04:00 2026-07-20T13:37:53-04:00 A judge partially granted the request from a dozen state attorneys general to temporarily place the $110 billion merger of Paramount and Warner Bros. Discovery on hold, as reported by Variety and Reuters . US District Judge Araceli Martínez-Olguín said that based on the new company's market share, "the Court is persuaded that it can presume the proposed merger is likely to violate antitrust laws," and that the states showed "irreparable harm" could occur without a temporary restraining order. A week ago , California, Arizona, Colorado, Connecticut, Massachusetts, Minnesota, Nevada, New Jersey, New Mexico, New York, Oregon, and Washington sued … Read the full story at The Verge. ]]> Sheena Vasani https://www.theverge.com/?p=967993 2026-07-20T12:34:24-04:00 2026-07-20T12:34:24-04:00 LG’s UltraGear Glossy OLED gaming monitor deliver deep, true-to-life blacks. | Image: LG If you’ve been thinking about upgrading your gaming monitor, LG’s 27-inch 27GX704A-B pairs a glossy WOLED panel with a fast refresh rate, and it’s currently on sale for $379.99 (about $70 off) at Amazon and directly from LG , marking a new low price. It originally launched at $799.99, but has been available for under $500 since May. LG UltraGear Glossy OLED gaming monitor (27-inches) Where to Buy: $449 $379.99 at Amazon $449 $379.99 at LG The monitor offers a 1440p resolution and a glossy coating, which isn’t a combination of specs we often see at this price. Most OLED monitors under $400 use QD-OLED panels, which can give blacks a slight bluish tint. LG’s WOLED monitor delivers truer-looking blacks while the glossy coating helps colors appear richer and more vibrant. It doesn’t sacrifice speed, either. The monitor boasts a fast 240Hz refresh rate with variable refresh rate support (Nvidia G-Sync compatible and AMD FreeSync Premium Pro), along with a fast 0.03ms response time to keep games looking sharp in motion.  It comes with a solid array of ports for the price, with two HDMI 2.1 ports, DisplayPort 1.4, and USB-A ports, making it easy to connect a PC, consoles, and accessories. Rounding things out, the stand is fully adjustable, too, allowing you to raise, lower, tilt, swivel, or rotate the display into portrait mode for a more comfortable setup. Or, because it has a VESA mount, you can yank it off and toss it onto a monitor arm of your choice. ]]> Stevie Bonifield https://www.theverge.com/?p=967983 2026-07-20T12:22:33-04:00 2026-07-20T12:14:58-04:00 This LG 34GX900A-B is one of
